tema: Past Simple — Regular Verbs (-ed) & Time Expressions

banco_oraciones.oracion: I watched a movie last night.
Sofía finished a big design project a week ago.
Kenji visited the Gold Museum two days ago.
Abuela Carmen danced all night on her last birthday.
Ben worked as a tour guide in Cartagena last winter.
I didn't work last Sunday. I stayed at home.
Sofía didn't travel last winter. She stayed in Medellín.
They didn't watch the game yesterday.
Kenji didn't study last night; he was very tired.
Did you watch the game last night?
Where did you travel last winter?
How did you celebrate your last birthday?
Did Kenji visit the museum two days ago?
Did you enjoy the party? Yes, I did.
Did Ben travel last Christmas? No, he didn't.
Did they play football yesterday? Yes, they did.

interaccion: Kenji: I visited the Gold Museum **two days ago**. It was amazing!
Sofía: **A week ago** I finished a big project. I celebrated with my family.
Ben: **Last Christmas** I stayed in a tiny hostel in Cartagena... I missed my family a lot.
Abuela Carmen: On **my last birthday** I danced all night. And **last winter** I traveled to the coast!

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I visit my grandma last week.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I visited my grandma last week.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: We use the past simple with -ed verbs to report finished actions at a specific past time — usually with a time expression like last night or two days ago.

filas: after soft (voiceless) sounds: p, k, s, sh, ch, f
after t and d — adds an extra syllable

ejercicios.pregunta: How do you pronounce the -ed in 'wanted'?

ejercicios.afirmacion: The -ed in 'played' sounds /ɪd/, with an extra syllable.
Time expressions like 'last night' and 'a week ago' usually go with the past simple.

ejercicios.texto: Sofía ___ (finish) her project a week ago.
We ___ (dance) all night at the party last Christmas.

ejercicios.respuesta: I visited my grandma last week
Did you enjoy the party last night

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I visit my grandma last week.
ejercicios.respuesta: I visited my grandma last week.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Ben didn't stayed at the hotel.
ejercicios.respuesta: Ben didn't stay at the hotel.

ejercicios.texto_con_error: Did you watched the movie last night?
ejercicios.respuesta: Did you watch the movie last night?

ejercicios.oracion_base: Did Kenji visit the museum two days ago?
Abuela Carmen didn't travel last winter.

ejercicios.pregunta: Did you study English last night? (short answer)
Did your friends visit you last weekend? (short answer)

guion: Sofía: Kenji, what did you do last weekend?
Kenji: I visited a coffee farm near Medellín. I loved it!
Sofía: Did you try the coffee?
Kenji: Yes, I did! I talked with the farmers and watched the whole process.
Sofía: I stayed at home. I cleaned my apartment and watched a movie.
Sofía: Yes, I did. Sometimes a quiet weekend is perfect.

comprension.pregunta: Where did Kenji go last weekend?
What did Sofía do last weekend?

reading.texto: Last Christmas, Ben stayed in Cartagena. He rented a small room in the old city and worked as a tour guide for two weeks. Every morning, he talked with tourists from many countries. Two days ago, he called his family in England: 'I missed you last Christmas,' he said, 'but I really enjoyed my time here. The people treated me like family.' Last night, Sofía and Kenji organized a small party for him, and they danced until midnight.

preguntas.afirmacion: Ben stayed in Cartagena last Christmas.
Ben worked as a taxi driver.
Ben called his family two days ago.
The party finished early in the evening.
